solution.p: We will factor r^{2}+4r-5 where a=1, b=4, and c=-5.
Mental Method: When we factor a polynomial in the form of x^2+bx+c, x^2+bxy+cy^2, or x^2y^2+bxy+c we can use mental math to factor. We need to find two numbers whose product is c, and whose sum is b.
For r^{2}+4r-5, since the product of -1 and 5 is -5, and the sum of -1 and 5 is 4, the factorization is \begin{aligned}[t]r^{2}+4r-5\amp =\left(r-1\right)\mathopen{}\left(r+5\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (1) \cdot (-5) = -5.

The number -5 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{lll}1(-5)\amp -1(5)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 4, and this sum happens with 5+(-1)=4 So we write \begin{aligned}[t]r^{2}+4r-5\amp =r^{2}+5r-r-5\newline \amp =r\mathopen{}\left(r+5\right)-\left(r+5\right)\newline \amp =\left(r-1\right)\mathopen{}\left(r+5\right)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in r^{2}+4r-5 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 1, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(1)\amp \end{array}
The third term, -5, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(-5)\amp -1(5)\amp \end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with r^{2}+4r-5 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]r^{2}+4r-5\amp =\left(r-1\right)\mathopen{}\left(r+5\right)\end{aligned}
